texto: The Servant who really studies his Master gradually becomes like his master
autor: gradually learns that he himself is the one who in the end does all the work and has all the power.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a gradually learns that he himself is the one who in the end does all the work and has all the power.: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “The Servant who really studies his Master gradually becomes like his master” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, don-cupitt, theology-s-strange-return pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de theology-s-strange-return.
